examples.title: Calculate Average Waiting Time
examples.description: Algorithm to compute average waiting time for given processes using FCFS order
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateAverageWaitingTime
INPUT: Array of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Average waiting time

BEGIN
  n ← number of processes
  waitingTime[0] ← 0
  totalWaiting ← 0
  
  // Calculate waiting time for each process (FCFS order)
  FOR i FROM 1 TO n-1 DO
    waitingTime[i] ← waitingTime[i-1] + burstTime[i-1]
    totalWaiting ← totalWaiting + waitingTime[i]
  END FOR
  
  avgWaiting ← totalWaiting / n
  RETURN avgWaiting
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates waiting time for each process assuming First-Come-First-Served scheduling. Each process waits for all previous processes to complete. The total waiting time is accumulated and divided by number of processes to get average.

examples.title: Calculate Turnaround Time
examples.description: Python function to compute turnaround time for processes
examples.code:
def calculate_turnaround_time(burst_time, waiting_time):
    """
    Calculate turnaround time for each process.
    
    Parameters:
    burst_time: list of burst times
    waiting_time: list of waiting times
    
    Returns:
    List of turnaround times
    """
    n = len(burst_time)
    turnaround_time = []
    
    for i in range(n):
        # Turnaround Time = Waiting Time + Burst Time
        tt = waiting_time[i] + burst_time[i]
        turnaround_time.append(tt)
    
    return turnaround_time

# Example usage
burst_time = [10, 5, 8]
waiting_time = [0, 10, 15]
turnaround = calculate_turnaround_time(burst_time, waiting_time)
print("Turnaround times:", turnaround)
print("Average:", sum(turnaround)/len(turnaround))
examples.explanation: This Python function calculates turnaround time using the fundamental formula: Turnaround Time = Waiting Time + Burst Time. It iterates through each process and adds its burst time to its waiting time.

examples.title: CPU Utilization Calculator
examples.description: C program to calculate CPU utilization percentage
examples.explanation: This C program calculates CPU utilization as a percentage. It divides the CPU busy time by total observation time and multiplies by 100. A CPU utilization of 75% means the CPU was busy executing processes for 75% of the observed period.

examples.title: Throughput Calculation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateThroughput
INPUT: completedProcesses, timeUnit
OUTPUT: Throughput value

BEGIN
  // Throughput = Number of processes completed / Time unit
  throughput ← completedProcesses / timeUnit
  
  // Example: If 50 processes complete in 10 seconds
  // Throughput = 50 / 10 = 5 processes per second
  
  RETURN throughput
END

ALGORITHM CompareSchedulingAlgorithms
INPUT: Two sets of processes with their completion times
OUTPUT: Comparison of throughput

BEGIN
  throughput1 ← CalculateThroughput(n1, time1)
  throughput2 ← CalculateThroughput(n2, time2)
  
  IF throughput1 > throughput2 THEN
    PRINT "Algorithm 1 has higher throughput"
  ELSE
    PRINT "Algorithm 2 has higher throughput"
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: Throughput measures how many processes the system completes per unit time. Higher throughput indicates the system is processing work more efficiently. This algorithm compares throughput between different scheduling approaches.
